seoTitle: How to Mail Your Tax Return From TurboTax in 2026
metaDescription: Learn how to print and mail your TurboTax tax return correctly in 2026, with tips to avoid mistakes and keep track of your submission.
intro: If you're not e-filing, getting your paper return in the mail correctly still matters — a mistake here can delay processing or trigger penalties. This walkthrough covers how to prepare, print, and mail your tax return from TurboTax for the 2026 filing season, whether you're submitting a personal or business return. It's aimed at anyone who wants to avoid the common slip-ups that come with printed tax paperwork.

steps.heading: Navigate to the mailing option in TurboTax
steps.body: Find where TurboTax lets you switch from e-filing to a printed, mailed return so you can prepare your documents correctly.

steps.heading: Prepare your tax return for mailing
steps.body: Review your completed return to make sure all forms and schedules are included before printing.

steps.heading: Print your tax forms
steps.body: Print the full set of forms TurboTax generates, keeping accuracy in mind since a paper filing won't catch errors the way e-file does.

steps.heading: Follow best practices for mailing your documents
steps.body: Use the tips covered for addressing, packaging, and sending your return to make sure it reaches the IRS or your state agency without issues.

steps.heading: Keep track of your mailed return
steps.body: Hold onto copies and any mailing confirmation so you have a record in case you need to verify submission later.

faq.question: Why would I mail my tax return instead of e-filing?
faq.answer: Some filers choose or need to mail a paper return rather than e-file, and this tutorial focuses specifically on getting that process right for both personal and business returns.

faq.question: What mistakes should I avoid when mailing my TurboTax return?
faq.answer: The video highlights accuracy checks and mailing tips designed to help you avoid common errors that could cause delays or compliance issues.

faq.question: How do I know my mailed return was received?
faq.answer: Keeping track of your mailed documents is covered as a best practice, since a mailed return doesn't give you the instant confirmation e-filing does.

faq.question: Does this process work the same for business tax returns?
faq.answer: Yes, the guide addresses best practices for both personal and business tax returns when it comes to printing and mailing from TurboTax.
